LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS IMPARK PARKING U.S., LLC, SAN FRANCISCO BASEBALL ASSOCIATES LLC AND CHINA BASIN BASEBALL COMPANY LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. THE PARTY ASSERTING LACK OF GOOD FAITH SHALL HAVE THE BURDEN OF PROOF ON THAT ISSUE. (CCP SECTION 877.6(D).) ONCE THE SETTLEMENT IS ATTACKED AS LACKING GOOD FAITH, HOWEVER, THE SETTLING PARTIES ARE REQUIRED TO FILE COUNTER-AFFIDAVITS TO MAKE AN EVIDENTIARY SHOWING THAT THE SETTLEMENT WAS "IN THE BALLPARK." (SEE MATTCO FORGE, INC. V. ARTHUR YOUNG & CO. (1995) 38 CAL.APP.4TH 1337, 1350, N. 6.) THERE MUST BE SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT A CRITICAL ASSUMPTION AS TO THE NATURE AND EXTENT OF THE SETTLING PARTIES' LIABILITY. (IBID.) THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED BY THE SETTLING PARTIES AND BY HILTON MANAGEMENT, LLC, INDICATES THAT THE $50,000 AMOUNT IS WITHIN THE BALLPARK. (SEE TECH-BILT, INC. V. WOODWARD-CLYDE & ASSOCIATES (1985) 38 CAL.3D 488.) PLAINTIFF HAS INCURRED $13,000 IN MEDICAL EXPENSES AND IT IS PROJECTED THAT PLAINTIFF WILL REQUIRE $20,000 OF ADDITIONAL TREATMENT. ADDITIONALLY, THE $50,000 AMOUNT IS PROPORTIONATE TO PLAINTIFF'S INITIAL SETTLEMENT REQUEST OF $75,000. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-27-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Aternatively, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HILTON MANAGEMENT, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Aternatively, Summary Adjudication is off calendar. The moving party was dismissed from the case on February 10, 2016. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dan McCrary telephone number 916-396-5922 fo Law Offices of Beverly Narayan for Defendants.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS IMPARK PARKING U.S., LLC, SAN FRANCISCO BASEBALL ASSOCIATES LLC AND CHINA BASIN BASEBALL COMPANY LLC'S Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement is granted. The party asserting lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue. (CCP section 877.6(d).) Once the settlement is attacked as lacking good faith, however, the settling parties are required to file counter-affidavits to make an evidentiary showing that the settlement was "in the ballpark." (See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n. 6.) There must be substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of the settling parties' liability. (Ibid.) The evidence presented by the settling parties and by Hilton Management, LLC, indicates that the $50,000 amount is within the ballpark. (See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488.) Plaintiff has incurred $13,000 in medical expenses and it is projected that Plaintiff will require $20,000 of additional treatment. Additionally, the $50,000 amount is proportionate to Plaintiff's initial settlement request of $75,000. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT HILTON MANAGEMENT, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant is the entity that set up the tailgating event including the food stand immediately next to the low chain where the accident occurred. (Contrearas Depo, 22:4-5; Plaintiff's Depo, 33:20-35:20). There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant created the dangerous condition by placing the food stand immediately next to the low chain and/or whether defendant should have warned of the hazard or taken precautions to prevent the incident. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
